bead.title: work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Hidden lifecycle model calls in `ddx work --watch` can mutate the operator's project checkout before a bead attempt starts. Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.
`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.
`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.

PROPOSED FIX
Run all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add `TestPreClaimIntakeHookDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving the readiness lifecycle call does not send the project root as the agent `WorkDir` and uses the intended read-only/no-write lifecycle boundary.
2. Add `TestPreClaimDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving `runPreClaimDecomposer` dispatches outside the project root while still returning validated child proposals from JSON output.
3. Add `TestPostLadderDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving `runDecomposer` dispatches outside the project root and only DDx store code creates children/updates the parent after validated output.
4. Add a dirty-guard regression test (for example `TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation`) where a lifecycle dispatch attempts to write a sentinel file in the project root; the hook must not leave the project root dirty and must return a non-success lifecycle result.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimIntakeHookDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestPreClaimDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestPostLadderDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
